question.text: For an option SELLER (writer), theta has what effect on their position?

answer.text: Positive effect - the writer benefits from time decay as the option loses value over time
Negative effect - the writer suffers from time decay
No effect - theta only impacts option buyers
Variable effect - it depends on whether the option is a call or put

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For option SELLERS (writers): - Theta works in their FAVOR (positive theta position) - Each passing day, the option loses time value - If the underlying doesn't move, the option becomes cheaper for the writer to buy back - The writer keeps more of the premium as time passes
**The Trap:** Theta is negative for BUYERS (time works against them) but positive for SELLERS.
**Cross-Exam Context:** "Sell options and let time decay work for you" is a common writing strategy.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Buyer: negative theta (time enemy). Seller: positive theta (time is friend).
